gracefulDegradation.evidence: Design system available — clean load path. Fallback chain well-structured in prompt.

questionQuality.evidence: AskUserQuestion format followed with re-ground, simplify, recommend, options.

questionEconomy.evidence: 2 questions before work. Viewport question borderline unnecessary — escape hatch exists but STOP overrides it.

designerLanguage.evidence: User-facing output uses design terms, not API jargon.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Correctly produces a question, not a plan — but output is incomplete by design for this turn.

nextStepGuidance.evidence: Implicit next step (answer the question), no explicit guidance.

briefComprehension.evidence: Correctly rates brief ~3/10, triggers clarification path.

iaPresentation.evidence: Correctly defers IA — doesn't present premature IA for vague brief.

componentCoverage.evidence: Not testable this turn. Structure supports >80% target.

textSpecificity.evidence: Not testable this turn. Requirement at Step 4 prevents placeholders.

edgeCaseCoverage.evidence: Not testable yet. Step 4 mandates empty/loading/error states.

antiSlop.evidence: Refusing to guess IS the anti-slop mechanism working.

planJsonValidity.evidence: No JSON yet. Format spec is comprehensive.

plan-design-02.name: Rich brief but no design system data

gracefulDegradation.evidence: Tries figma_get_design_system_kit before hard-stopping. Proceeds with whatever data it gets.

questionQuality.evidence: Questions about genuine design choices (deactivation confirmation).

questionEconomy.evidence: Skips clarification (brief is rich). 2 questions max.

designerLanguage.evidence: Uses team members, role badge, invite button — not figmaKey/nodeId.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Plan covers all elements from brief.

nextStepGuidance.evidence: Explicitly says 'Run /build-design to execute this plan.'

briefComprehension.evidence: Captures all 5 columns, 3 roles, 3 statuses, 3 admin actions, search, filter.

iaPresentation.evidence: Clear IA tree with hierarchy stated.

componentCoverage.evidence: Coverage achievable but figmaKey resolution degraded without local data.

textSpecificity.evidence: Specific text: 'Invite team member', 'Search by name or email'.

edgeCaseCoverage.evidence: Plans empty team, no results, deactivation confirmation.

antiSlop.evidence: Uses table layout as specified, not a card grid.

planJsonValidity.evidence: Valid structure but incomplete figmaKeys without local data.

plan-design-03.name: Screenshot of complex dashboard — visual parsing + anti-slop

gracefulDegradation.evidence: Clean load path with design system available.

questionQuality.evidence: Questions about ambiguous screenshot elements are reasonable.

questionEconomy.evidence: 2 questions. Viewport question forced by STOP despite obvious desktop context.

designerLanguage.evidence: Mostly designer-friendly. Token-built elements may involve technical frame language.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Covers ~15 elements. Sparklines and progress bars less detailed as token-built.

briefComprehension.evidence: Captures most elements. 1-2 subtle details may be missed.

iaPresentation.evidence: Clear IA with hierarchy: metrics first → filters → table.

componentCoverage.evidence: ~60% coverage. Sparklines, stat cards, progress bars are token-built.

textSpecificity.evidence: Uses exact values from screenshot: '526', '2:24', 'Site traffic'.

edgeCaseCoverage.evidence: Happy-path screenshot. Some sections may get thin edge case coverage.

antiSlop.evidence: Follows screenshot layout, not generic grid. Risk of stat cards looking like generic cards.

planJsonValidity.evidence: Valid structure. Large JSON with more room for omissions.

expectedBehaviors.Plans dark theme using token mode: FAIL — No dark theme detection in prompt

brainstorm-01.name: Simple settings page — meaningful variation vs cosmetic shuffling

questionQuality.evidence: AskUserQuestion format followed. Starting point question slightly verbose.

questionEconomy.evidence: Constraints question is formulaic — asked every time even when user mentioned none.

designerLanguage.evidence: Designer-friendly. Internal figmaKey references don't leak to user.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Plan JSONs, built frames, screenshots, comparison with recommendations.

nextStepGuidance.evidence: 4 specific next steps with exact commands including /content-stress.

variationDiversity.evidence: Strong guards against cosmetic variation. Thesis requirement forces articulation.

scamperApplication.evidence: Evaluates all 7 lenses, selects distinct ones, requires lens in plan JSON.

jtbdAlignment.evidence: Each variation requires user job. Allows 'same job, different lens' — slight loophole.

recommendationQuality.evidence: Explicitly bans aesthetic language. Behavior-grounded example provided.

buildability.evidence: Full plan JSON with components, tokens, text. Directly executable.

questionQuality.evidence: Redundantly asks about constraints the user already provided.

questionEconomy.evidence: 3 questions before work. Constraints question is redundant.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Plans buildable but constraint relaxation not documented in output format.

variationDiversity.evidence: SCAMPER produces diverse approaches but constraint pressure could collapse diversity.

scamperApplication.evidence: Same rigor. JTBD table mentions pricing directly.

jtbdAlignment.evidence: Pricing naturally maps to Decide. Limited natural JTBD diversity.

contradictionHandling.evidence: Has a section but only 2 lines. Right approach, critically underspecified.

recommendationQuality.evidence: Behavior-grounding requirement strong. No pricing-specific guidance.

buildability.evidence: Plans buildable but interactive states (progressive disclosure) not in schema.

gracefulDegradation.evidence: Warns about missing data but proceeds. Does NOT try figma_get_design_system_kit first — a gap.

questionEconomy.evidence: 4 questions total. Feedback source necessary. 'Include resolved?' could be defaulted.

designerLanguage.evidence: Plain design language throughout. No API jargon in user-facing text.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Before/after screenshots, classification table, deferred items. No structured JSON output.

nextStepGuidance.evidence: Mentions /plan-design for scope changes. No explicit end-of-revision guidance.

classificationAccuracy.evidence: All 5 comments correctly classified: spacing=principle, purple=preference, save=usability, export=scope, overlap=bug.

priorityOrder.evidence: Explicit priority: Usability > Principle > Content > Bug > Preference > Scope.

preferenceHandling.evidence: Never applies preference changes without asking. Airtight.

scopeRecognition.evidence: Export correctly flagged as scope change and deferred to /plan-design.

gracefulDegradation.evidence: Same gap — warns without figma_get_design_system_kit fallback.

questionQuality.evidence: Minimal questions for this simple case.

questionEconomy.evidence: Skips feedback source question. Only asks to approve revision plan.

designerLanguage.evidence: Responds in same natural language as the input.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Before/after screenshots, revised frame with labeling.

nextStepGuidance.evidence: Could be more explicit about next steps when no deferred items.

skipUnnecessaryQuestions.evidence: Explicitly handles inline feedback — skips source question.

surgicalPrecision.evidence: Cardinal rule: modify, don't rebuild. Diff-plan requires specific from/to values.

nonDestructive.evidence: Non-destructive execution labeled MANDATORY. Clones frame first.

screenshotEconomy.evidence: Batch all changes, single verification screenshot. Per-change only for complex changes.

plan-component-01.name: Duplicate risk — notification banner vs Alert

questionEconomy.evidence: Duplicate resolution is essential. Potential over-questioning in Steps 2-3.

duplicateDetection.evidence: Full Step 1 devoted to this. 'Non-negotiable' blocking. Searches by function.

searchByFunction.evidence: Explicitly: 'Think about what the component DOES, not what it's called.'

blockBeforeProceeding.evidence: 'STOP. Wait for response. This is non-negotiable.'

existingCapabilityPresentation.evidence: Shows matches but doesn't mandate full variant/prop display.

plan-component-02.name: Complex data table row — variant architecture

questionEconomy.evidence: Multiple decisions needed. Some have obvious answers per PRINCIPLES.md.

designerLanguage.evidence: Uses design terms: compact/comfortable, not px heights.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Plan captures variant axes, booleans, swap slots, relationships.

variantArchitecture.evidence: Correct mechanism: booleans for selectable/expandable, swaps for cell content.

matrixManagement.evidence: Reduces to Height(3) x State(4) = 12. Well under 20 threshold.

conventionMatching.evidence: Studies existing table components. 'Similar complexity or category.'

compositionAwareness.evidence: Plans containedBy relationship with Table. Missing interface details.

gracefulDegradation.evidence: 3-tier degradation with figma_get_design_system_kit before suggesting extraction.

questionQuality.evidence: Combines scope AND strictness into one question. No explicit RECOMMENDATION.

questionEconomy.evidence: 2 questions total. No escape hatch for obvious defaults.

designerLanguage.evidence: Uses design terms. 'nodeId' appears in JSON schema.

outputCompleteness.evidence: Comprehensive report. No 'top 3 fixes' priority summary.

detectionRate.evidence: Framework covers all 6 issues. Miller's vs Hick's overlap could cause confusion.

severityAccuracy.evidence: Explicit thresholds: <44px=critical, >12 ungrouped=critical. Error jargon may be Warning not Critical.

heuristicCorrectness.evidence: Correct heuristic mapping. Findings section categorized by type, not heuristic — could disconnect.

fixSpecificity.evidence: Some examples specific ('Use spacing.4'). Not mandated for every suggestion.

overallScoreCalibration.evidence: Weighted average could produce ~5 despite multiple criticals. No 'critical floor' rule. Supportive tone risks inflation.

questionQuality.evidence: Depth question follows format. No explicit AskUserQuestion mandate section.

designerLanguage.evidence: Design-professional language. Some technical terms (WCAG, CSS pseudo-class).

outputCompleteness.evidence: Visual score bars, issues, strengths, evidence, JSON report.

scoreCalibration.evidence: Scoring guides produce ~3.6 weighted average. Well-calibrated. Tone creates slight inflation risk.

honestAssessment.evidence: Rubrics force low scores. Hard to inflate with explicit guides.

specificity.evidence: Mandatory evidence citing layers, properties, tokens by name.

actionability.evidence: 'What would make it a 10' required. May not always include exact token paths.

criticalGaps.gap: No dark theme detection from screenshots
criticalGaps.impact: FAIL on dark-themed dashboard test case
criticalGaps.suggestedFix: Add a step to detect theme from visual input and apply matching token mode

criticalGaps.gap: Contradictory constraints handling is only 2 lines
criticalGaps.suggestedFix: Expand to 10+ lines with enumeration, creative resolution examples, tradeoff framing

criticalGaps.gap: Constraints question lacks escape hatch for inline constraints
criticalGaps.impact: Redundantly asks about constraints user already provided
criticalGaps.suggestedFix: Skip constraints question when user already stated constraints in message

criticalGaps.impact: Weighted average could give passing score despite multiple critical issues
criticalGaps.suggestedFix: Add rule: 'If any dimension has a Critical finding, overall score cannot exceed 5'

criticalGaps.gap: 'Lead with strengths' instruction conflicts with honest assessment
criticalGaps.impact: Anti-pattern triggered: frontloads praise for mediocre component
criticalGaps.suggestedFix: Change to 'Lead with the most impactful finding, whether positive or negative'

criticalGaps.gap: Missing figma_get_design_system_kit fallback in data loading
criticalGaps.impact: Warns about missing data without trying silent fallback first
